headline: FIRST-BASELINE assessment for Mauritius (rotation queue, never previously assessed). Published composite 62.5 (Established band, rank 32). Research finds modest negative drift driven by: (1) Mauritius failed to appear before African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights at both 81st (Nov 2024) and 85th (Oct 2025) Sessions to present its 11th Periodic Report — a transparency/accountability gap; (2) ongoing Chagossian rights gap, with March 2026 BIOT court ruling affirming right of abode and April 2026 ruling overturning unlawful immigration restrictions revealing decades-long failure to honor Chagossian rights; (3) mandatory SIM re-registration (Oct 2023) and attempted social media block during Nov 2024 election remain unaddressed civil-liberty concerns. Counter-signals: Ramgoolam government released candid State of the Economy audit (Dec 2024), 2023 Supreme Court struck down sodomy laws. Net assessment: composite of 60.0 vs published 62.5 — sub-threshold delta of -2.5 pts; band remains Established. Confirmation, no proposal applied.
key_evidence: Mauritius failed to appear before African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights to present its 11th Periodic Report at both the 81st (Nov 2024) and 85th (Oct 2025) Ordinary Sessions — a recurring accountability gap (CHR Centre, 2026).

Chagossian rights: March 2026 BIOT court affirmed right of abode; April 2026 BIOT Supreme Court overturned decades-old immigration restrictions — Mauritius and UK have not resolved long-standing Chagossian poverty, stigma, and discrimination concerns (HRW Mar 2025).
April 2026: Chagos sovereignty deal stalled after Trump criticism; treaty ratification incomplete.
October 2023 SIM re-registration mandate and Nov 2024 attempted social media block during election remain unaddressed (BTI 2026).
Counter-signal: 2023 Supreme Court struck down sodomy laws (positive equity signal, but LGBT individuals still lack legal recognition for same-sex relationships per HRW 2025).
Counter-signal: Ramgoolam government published State of the Economy audit (Dec 2024) revealing previously understated fiscal deficit — transparency positive.

evidence.source: Centre for Human Rights, University of Pretoria
evidence.finding: Mauritius prepared its 11th Periodic Report covering Aug 2020-Apr 2024 but failed to appear before the African Commission to present it at both the 81st Ordinary Session (Nov 2024) and 85th Ordinary Session (Oct 2025). Recurring accountability gap.

Chagossians forcibly displaced and continue to suffer poverty, stigma, and discrimination; LGBT people lack legal recognition; Internet/social media restrictions incompatible with international human rights law.
BIOT court rulings (Mar/Apr 2026) affirm Chagossian right of abode and overturn unlawful immigration restrictions; Chagos sovereignty deal complications continue.
Mauritius experienced severe deterioration in freedom of expression and civil liberties (mandatory SIM re-registration Oct 2023, attempted social media block Nov 2024).

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.4 (-0.1). Mauritius operates structural human-rights detection mechanisms (National Human Rights Commission Annual Report 2024 published Mar 2025) — A1 anchor 4 sustained — but failure to appear before African Commission is a downstream signal of incomplete suffering-detection follow-through.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.4 (-0.1). Some structural empathy mechanisms in place; LGBT progress (sodomy decriminalization) but ongoing lack of recognition gap suggests E5 cultural empathy not yet at anchor 4.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.4 (-0.1). General responsiveness functional; Chagossian rights response remains inadequate over decades.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.8 (-0.2). Chagossian rights gap is a multi-decade EQ5 historical-harm-acknowledgment failure; LGBT recognition incomplete; near-universal coverage in core social services preserves higher EQ1 score but not enough to offset.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.8 (-0.2). Strong in B1/B5 fundamentals (functional democracy, no widespread coercion) but B3 Scope Clarity issues from SIM re-registration and social media block attempts.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.2 (-0.3). AB3 Transparency: failure to appear at African Commission to present required Periodic Report is a structural transparency failure, recurring at two consecutive sessions; partially offset by State of the Economy audit transparency.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.4 (-0.1). Standard institutional planning capacity; no extraordinary structural critique or coalitional compassion evidence.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.3 (-0.2). I4 Values Alignment: stated commitment to multilateral engagement contradicted by ACHPR no-shows; I5 Resilience of Care preserved through change of government.

math_check.validator_check: drift -4.1 pts within ceiling but band-boundary issue noted

band_boundary_note: Calculated composite 58.4 crosses the 60-point Established/Functional boundary. However: (a) confidence is LOW because this is a first-baseline rotation assessment; (b) most negative evidence is out-of-window (pre-2026-04-18); (c) Mauritius retains strong democratic institutions, free judiciary, and social-services coverage that would be impaired if the score change were applied without further differentiation evidence. Recommendation: flag-for-review, do not auto-apply. A post-flag follow-up cycle should validate or reject the band crossing.
negativeSignals: ACHPR Periodic Report no-shows (recurring); Chagossian rights gap (multi-decade); SIM re-registration and attempted social media restriction; LGBT recognition incomplete.
positiveSignals: Functional democracy with peaceful change of government; Supreme Court 2023 sodomy decriminalization; State of the Economy audit transparency; National Human Rights Commission active.
sector_context: Mauritius is the highest-ranked African country in the published index (rank 32). First-baseline rotation cycle establishes evidence pattern; baseline score appears modestly overstated in BND/ACC/EQU but not by enough to confidently move band on first cycle.
open_actions_resolved: Chagossian rights gap captured as EQU/EQ5 evidence
open_actions_carried: Re-queue Mauritius for second rotation cycle to validate or reject band-boundary signal
Monitor for 11th Periodic Report submission to ACHPR at 86th Session
